items.headline: City Attorney position to be filled; litigation with environmental group ongoing
items.summary: Mountain View is appointing a new City Attorney. The city is also defending against a lawsuit filed by San Francisco Baykeeper (an environmental advocacy organization) in federal court, alongside Sunnyvale, regarding an unspecified environmental or water-related claim.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study Housing Element update
items.summary: The Planning Commission scheduled a study session on updates to Cupertino's Housing Element. The agenda document cuts off before detailing what changes are being proposed, so no specifics are available.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study artificial turf policy
items.summary: The Planning Commission will hold a study session on an artificial turf policy. The specific details of what is being proposed or studied were not provided in the agenda.

items.headline: Industrial and Financial Systems opens office on Page Mill Road
items.summary: IFS, a professional services company, received a permit to install signage for a 6,000+ square foot tenant space on the second floor of 425 Page Mill Rd.

items.headline: Foxconn receives $2.4M permit for tool installation at Automation Park
items.summary: The semiconductor equipment manufacturer is permitted to install tooling at its facility on Automation Parkway. The project cost suggests expansion of production capacity at one of San José's largest employers in the tech sector.

items.headline: Five ADU permits issued on same day across San José neighborhoods
items.summary: The city issued permits for five separate accessory dwelling units (conversions and new builds) on June 17, ranging from $50K to $179K. The cluster suggests accelerating adoption of ADU development as a housing supply strategy.

items.headline: City acquires property at 26-36 South First Street
items.summary: San José City Council took action on the acquisition of real property at 26-36 South First Street. The agenda does not specify the purchase price, intended use, or development plans, so the concrete impact to residents cannot be determined from this source.

items.headline: Housing Department Loan Portfolio Audit Report released
items.summary: The council received an audit report on the Housing Department's loan portfolio. The agenda does not provide findings, recommendations, or details about loan performance or outcomes.
